mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically compressing valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid deterioration in the labor market signals an impending recession, triggering downward earnings revisions and risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the cross-sectional IC remains positive during labor market deterioration.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial output and housing construction indicates a supply-demand imbalance; when manufacturing activity accelerates while residential investment contracts, inventory accumulation pressures corporate margins, leading to short-term negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Cross-sectional IC remains positive during INDPRO-HOUST divergence periods, or 5-day forward returns do not decline when INDPRO growth exceeds HOUST growth by more than 1 standard deviation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising household credit balances reflect elevated consumer leverage; in a high-inflation environment, this accelerates spending but forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, eventually compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when RSAFS monthly growth exceeds 2% alongside CPIAUCSL values above 240, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns in backtesting over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Steady payroll expansion supports corporate revenue expectations and consumer spending power, driving positive short-term equity momentum as earnings growth forecasts are revised upward.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline during payroll expansion phases, or the rolling Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Sub-1.5% quarterly GDP growth signals economic deceleration, prompting institutional investors to reduce equity exposure and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter as earnings revisions turn negative.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 1.5%
failure_condition: Rank IC remains above 0.05 during GDP deceleration episodes, or forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains elevated while unemployment declines, it indicates a lagged tightening effect that will eventually break the labor market; this policy lag triggers risk-off positioning and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive when FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.2% and UNRATE is falling, or the interaction term fails to explain more than 5% of variance in 20-day returns during tightening phases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial output above 105 indicates robust manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, which historically precedes positive equity momentum in early-cycle expansions as corporate margins expand.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative when INDPRO exceeds 105 and PAYEMS growth is positive, or the signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.
